latin: Sortes tibi semper faveant! hoc dicunt antequam liberi in arenam mittuntur. verba blanda sunt sed res ipsa crudelis est — liberi inter se pugnare debent.

questions.prompt: What is the famous wish before the Games?
questions.target: May the odds be ever in your favor
choices: May the odds be ever in your favor

questions.prompt: What is cruel about the situation?

translation: May the odds be ever in your favor! This is said before the children are sent into the arena. The words are pleasant but the reality is cruel — children must fight one another.
